title: 1.1: Reports building kitty produces four compiled .so files (fast_data_types.so, glfw-x11.so, glfw-wayland.so, rsync.so) to verify comprehensive build output enumeration.
1.3:Identifies kitty.fast_data_types load as C extension during test execution to demonstrate understanding of Python import mechanism.
1.4: Identifies kittens.transfer.rsync load as C extension during test execution to demonstrate understanding of Python import mechanisms.
1.5: Reports that the GLFW modules (kitty/glfw-x11.so and kitty/glfw-wayland.so) are not imported as Python extension modules during test execution.
1.6: Reports removing fast_data_types.so causes ModuleNotFoundError from kitty_tests.init.py blocking all tests to confirm framework initialization dependency.
1.7:Reports removing rsync.so causes ModuleNotFoundError from kitty_tests.file_transmission blocking all tests demonstrating the test discovery.
1.8: Reports fast_data_types.so removal result in executing zero tests, identifying it as critical extension.
1.9: Reports rsync.so removal result in executing zero tests, identifying it as critical extension.
1.10: Reports GLFW modules missing affects less than 2% of tests, identifying them as optional modules.
2.1: States that kitty compiler module(e.g. fast_data_types.so, rsync.so, glfw-x11.so) is critical based on naming or assumed purpose.
2.2: Claims GLFW modules are imported as standard Python C extension module during test execution.
